hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by forming favorable π-π stacking or cation-π interactions with ECL2 residues (e.g., Tyr195), which are absent in SSTR3/5. Simultaneously, mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target electrostatic complementarity (e.g., with SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved residues in TM5/TM6) while being tolerated by SSTR2's less acidic pocket.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is not sufficient; selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5 is critical. The next iteration should focus on disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to improve Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to form conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations on these positions can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore at positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14.

Introducing charged or aromatic residues at positions 1/2 and hydrophobic residues at positions 5/6 can disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Mutating positions 11/12 to smaller residues can reduce off-target footprint.
